nameOfCommonIngredientsGlossary: SR-(OLIGOPEPTIDE-163 SH-POLYPEPTIDE-117 SH-POLYPEPTIDE-94 SH-POLYPEPTIDE-95 PENTAPEPTIDE-63 OLIGOPEPTIDE-163 SH-POLYPEPTIDE-6 NONAPEPTIDE-43 SH-POLYPEPTIDE-6) DIMER
chemicalName: sr-(Oligopeptide-163 sh-Polypeptide-117 sh-Polypeptide-94 sh-Polypeptide-95 Pentapeptide-63 Oligopeptide-163 sh-Polypeptide-6 Nonapeptide-43 sh-Polypeptide-6) Dimer
chemicalDescription: This peptide is a dimer linked by a disulfide bridge between the cysteines, consisting of a single chain synthetic fusion peptide produced by fermentation in Chinese hamster ovary cells (CHO). The starting gene is synthesized by the fusion of the genes from Oligopeptide-163, sh-Polypeptide-117, sh-Polypeptide-94, sh-Polypeptide-95, Pentapeptide-63, sh-Polypeptide-6 and Nonapeptide-43. This fusion protein binds to Immunoglobulin E (IgE) and Interleukin-10 (IL-10) receptors; and it contains 433 amino acids which may contain disulfide bonds and/or glycosylation, and consists of the proper sequence of the 20 amino acids.
inciName: SR-(OLIGOPEPTIDE-163 SH-POLYPEPTIDE-117 SH-POLYPEPTIDE-94 SH-POLYPEPTIDE-95 PENTAPEPTIDE-63 OLIGOPEPTIDE-163 SH-POLYPEPTIDE-6 NONAPEPTIDE-43 SH-POLYPEPTIDE-6) DIMER
